engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is evaluating a physical PRODUCT line (compostable straws/cups) via material types, specs, certifications and sustainability proof — features + proof it works, not a person's expertise or a browsable visual portfolio. Store-redirect-flavored 'order in one click' CTAs sit atop a full feature/differentiator argument, so still thing.

goal.one_action: request a custom bulk quote (B2B order inquiry with volume + logo upload)

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page B2B manufacturer brochure: home pitch → product catalog with spec table → sustainability proof → company timeline → dedicated quote form.

mapped.observed_as: Custom Eco-Friendly Straws for the World
Companies that trust in our products
Ready to buy? / Transform Your Brand with Eco Revolution's Straws!
Polylactide / Wood Fiber / Compostable & Reusable / Polypropylene / Paper Straws
Get a quote & order now! / Contact

unmapped.why_no_fit: A dated company-history timeline is heritage/credibility storytelling; mapped to howItWorks as closest but it isn't a process — it's a chronology

components_needed: multi-field B2B quote form (with file upload)

confidence.why: fetch_quality full, all 6 pages captured with sections, forms and proof detailed; unambiguous product/manufacturer lead-gen archetype.

gap_findings: B2B bulk-quote form has volume tiers (+50k to +100m units) and a logo-upload field — richer than the generic lead-form capability implies, but still expressible as a multi-field form.
Heritage milestone timeline (founding-year chronology) has no dedicated canonical section; forced into howItWorks.
